description_long: This is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a straight, mid-mounted wing configuration and a conventional fuselage. The aircraft features two engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller, one with a red spinner and the other with a white spinner. The landing gear is a tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a single nose wheel. The tail section consists of a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, forming a conventional tail design. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, likely enclosed, though detailed canopy features are not visible. The airplane's exterior is primarily gray with some colored accents on the engines and landing gear.
description: A small, twin-propeller airplane with a straight mid-wing, tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail. The fuselage is gray with colored engine spinners and landing gear details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, tricycle landing gear, and the straight wings extending horizontally.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the mid-mounted wing, engine nacelles, and the vertical stabilizer at the tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the engines and landing gear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other engine nacelle, wing, and the vertical stabilizer.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit at the front of the fuselage, likely enclosed.
